title: Pregnancy danger signs
red_flags: vaginal bleeding during pregnancy or postpartum
seizure, fainting, or altered mental status
fever with pregnancy or postpartum concern
marked swelling of face or hands
escalation_criteria: Pregnancy or postpartum bleeding requires emergency escalation.
Seizure, severe headache with vision changes, or altered mental status requires emergency escalation.
local_actions: Escalate through the local obstetric, emergency, or clinician-supervisor pathway.
Document pregnancy status, gestational estimate if known, bleeding description, pain, vital signs, and timeline for handoff.
forbidden_actions: Do not provide medication, dosing, delivery, or procedure instructions unless a local clinician or protocol explicitly provides them.
safety_boundary: Prototype protocol navigation card for trained-responder review only. It is not a clinical guideline and cannot diagnose, prescribe, dose, triage autonomously, or override local protocol.
source_note: Prototype protocol card derived from public guideline concepts. Not a clinical guideline.
